abortController_future_dkr.activation_condition: Only fund this separate DKR if accepted component measurements leave a material allocation gap or stack attribution points to AbortController.
abortController_future_dkr.candidate_question: Can signal ownership become lazy without changing parent/caller cancellation, abort reason identity, stream termination, or cleanup order?
abortController_future_dkr.source_observation: AbortController remains eagerly allocated once per context in both baseline and candidate: 2, 3, and 5 instances per operation across the three rows.

candidate_next_measurement.method: Build each variant independently from exact head 293dcdf2d6d0b274418bcca8461f7eb122e10c52 and run the already accepted frozen throughput, sampled-allocation, retained-control, and cleanup protocol. Do not retune thresholds.
candidate_next_measurement.steering_rule: Compare each row against the same baseline. If V4 alone reproduces divergence, fund a smaller DKR split for cleanups, descendants, activeIterators, and children before a new PKR.

payload.claim_boundary: This is a read-only source and workload map. It ranks hypotheses and semantic risk only. It does not establish allocation bytes, throughput, retained memory, cleanup correctness, target attainment, wall hold, or release readiness. Independent validation is required before the orchestrator accepts the learning.
payload.decision_target: Choose separable source variants that can isolate the managed_ctx_exec and sdk_session_run allocation regressions without authorizing unrelated lifecycle work.
questions_answered: The three workload rows create 2, 3, and 5 ExecutionContextImpl instances per operation.
resourceListeners and resourceControllers are never materialized by any row.
scope_run and managed_ctx_exec materialize the same three candidate collections per operation: resources, descendants, and children; they differ in context count and repeated hot-path transitions.
sdk_session_run uniquely materializes ExecutionContextImpl.cleanups and activeIterators and has four parent contexts that materialize children.
The current diff splits into four compile-separable variants without touching AbortController.
questions_unanswered: Which variant causes the measured managed_ctx_exec and sdk_session_run allocation regressions.
Whether the allocation regression comes from candidate-side allocations, profiler attribution variance, or interactions between variants.
The byte weight of each omitted collection versus each added lazy branch and temporary close expression.
Whether a smaller split inside V4 is needed after the first separable measurements.
payload.required_sentence: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The source map does not support a performance-improvement claim.
The observed allocation ordering contradicts simple omitted-object counts, so AG-PERF-CLAIM requires independent variant measurements.
V3 and V4 touch AG-CLEANUP-EFFECT and AG-SESSION-ISOLATION and require the listed lifecycle and SDK gates.

row_component_table.baseline_rule: The baseline constructor eagerly allocates all seven lifecycle collections on every ExecutionContextImpl. Baseline counts are therefore the row context count for each collection.
row_component_table.count_unit: per workload operation; N operations multiply every count by N

children.exercising_contexts: R reuses one Set to register A and B sequentially

cleanups.exercising_contexts: none; the lease cleanup is stored on its ResourceEntry

managed_ctx_exec.context_tree: managed boundary R -> first scalar child A; R -> second scalar child B

descendants.exercising_contexts: R reuses one Set to track both sequential ctx.exec promises

resources.exercising_contexts: R owns and shares the default boundary-owned lease across A and B

seedTags.baseline: Allocates one blocked Set plus the spread and map arrays for each child even though no tags are supplied.
seedTags.candidate: Both calls return before blocked-set construction; zero blocked Sets.
seedTags.exercising_contexts: A and B are seeded from R.

cleanups.exercising_contexts: none; resource ctx.cleanup writes ResourceEntry.cleanups, not ExecutionContextImpl.cleanups

scope_run.context_tree: owner boundary R -> inline dependency child I

seedTags.baseline: Allocates one blocked Set plus the spread and map arrays even though execTags, blockedTags, and flowTags are absent.
seedTags.candidate: Returns before blocked-set construction; zero blocked Sets.

activeIterators.exercising_contexts: P owns the consumed dispatch execStream iterator

children.exercising_contexts: R registers S, S registers P, P registers D, and D registers T

cleanups.exercising_contexts: S registers the prepared context close callback; unregistering leaves the allocated array empty

sdk_session_run.context_tree: session boundary R -> sdk.session.run child S -> prepared dispatch context P -> dispatch child D -> selected turn child T

descendants.exercising_contexts: R tracks sdk.session.run, P tracks its execStream result, and D tracks turn.exec

resourceControllers.exercising_contexts: none; controller(dispatch) creates a flow handle, not a resource controller

resources.exercising_contexts: R owns sdk.session; S reuses the upward entry

seedTags.baseline: Allocates a blocked Set plus spread and map arrays for R->S, P->D, and D->T. P->D carries execution tags but dispatch has no flow tags, so the baseline still builds the blocked containers.
seedTags.candidate: All three calls return before blocked-set construction because every target flow has no flowTags.
seedTags.exercising_contexts: S, D, and T are seeded; P receives prepared tags directly.

common_required_gates: frozen lifecycle comparison replay for the selected variant

variant_table.expected_allocation_impact: medium to high by source count, but unmeasured
variant_table.expected_throughput_impact: low to medium; removes collection construction from every scalar child activation in these rows
variant_table.row_divergence_explanation: weak; source savings grow from scope to managed to SDK, opposite the observed allocation ordering
variant_table.semantic_risk: medium because tag precedence and blocked-tag behavior are changed
variant_table.source_count_delta: Avoids one Set and two temporary arrays per seedTags call in these rows: 1 call for scope_run, 2 for managed_ctx_exec, 3 for sdk_session_run.

variant_table.expected_allocation_impact: high by omitted constructor count, but unmeasured
variant_table.expected_throughput_impact: low in these rows because observer APIs are not called
hunks: field declarations for resourceListeners and resourceControllers within -2239,7/+2239,7
variant_table.row_divergence_explanation: weak; the omitted-map count is largest for SDK, while SDK allocation regressed
variant_table.semantic_risk: low to medium; controller identity and resource event delivery still require full gates
variant_table.source_count_delta: Neither map materializes in any row. The candidate omits 4 eager maps per scope_run operation, 6 per managed_ctx_exec operation, and 10 per sdk_session_run operation.

variant_table.expected_allocation_impact: medium by source count, but unmeasured
variant_table.expected_throughput_impact: medium because find, create, close, and release paths gain optional checks
variant_table.row_divergence_explanation: possible hot-path contributor but not proven; managed and SDK perform more upward lookups while their measured allocation regressed
variant_table.semantic_risk: high because boundary/current ownership, cached failure, release, and cleanup order depend on it
variant_table.source_count_delta: One resources map still materializes in every row; omitted maps are 1 per scope_run operation, 2 per managed_ctx_exec operation, and 4 per sdk_session_run operation.

variant_table.expected_allocation_impact: highest remaining lifecycle-state impact by source count, but unmeasured
variant_table.expected_throughput_impact: medium to high because child registration, promise tracking, iterator tracking, close, and cleanup paths gain lazy branches
hunks: field declarations for cleanups, descendants, activeIterators, and children within -2239,7/+2239,7
variant_table.row_divergence_explanation: strongest source-only candidate because managed and SDK exercise more child and descendant transitions, and SDK alone exercises prepared cleanups and active iterators; this is a hypothesis, not a performance claim
variant_table.semantic_risk: highest; structured cancellation, join order, stream return, close settlement, and cleanup order all depend on these fields
variant_table.source_count_delta: Omitted eager collections are 6 per scope_run operation, 10 per managed_ctx_exec operation, and 11 per sdk_session_run operation after subtracting actual materializations.
